summary: Impersonation package aliases legitimate dependency to attacker-scoped @torbeck/heap
details: @torbeck/priority-queue@6.3.9 reproduces the README, author metadata, repository URL, source code, and MIT copyright of @datastructures-js/priority-queue (by Eyas Ranjous) but is published under an unrelated @torbeck scope. Its package.json contains "dependencies": { "@datastructures-js/heap": "npm:@torbeck/heap@4.3.12" }, which uses npm's alias syntax to redirect the well-known @datastructures-js/heap name to @torbeck/heap@4.3.12. src/priorityQueue.js require()s '@datastructures-js/heap' on module load, so installing this package resolves and loads code from @torbeck/heap — a package under the impersonator's control — whenever the priority queue module is imported. The impersonation of a legitimate maintainer's identity combined with a dependency alias that silently substitutes a same-scope-controlled package for a trusted upstream is the lure/dropper shape: harm is delivered through the aliased dependency rather than through this tarball's own code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
